---

# UC-18.3.22 ¬∑ VXLAN Underlay Path MTU and DF Bit Fragmentation Risk

> **Criticality:** Medium &middot; **Difficulty:** Intermediate &middot; **Pillar:** Observability &middot; **Type:** Performance, Fault, Operations &middot; **Wave:** Crawl &middot; **Status:** Verified

*We check whether the hidden wiring between big switches leaves enough room for padded traffic, so messages are not quietly dropped when splitting them apart is not allowed.*

---

## Description

Fuses **NX-OS** **interface** **MTU** telemetry, orchestrated **path-MTU** discovery, **NVE** encap/**DF** counters, **NX-OS** **syslog** lines that signal **ICMP need-fragment** / **too-big** conditions, and **DF-bit** **ping** probes‚Äîsurfacing **`mtu_value`**, **`vxlan_overhead`** (expected **~50 B** on **IPv4** **VXLAN**), **`pmtu_detected`**, and **`df_drops`** so operators see **underlay** segments that are too tight before **east-west** throughput collapses or **TCP** gets stuck behind **PMTUD** black holes.

## Value

When the **underlay** cannot carry **jumbo**-sized **VXLAN** frames end to end, applications see **random** **slowness**, **iSCSI** **timeouts**, or **backup** jobs that **crawl**‚Äîsymptoms that often masquerade as **server** or **storage** defects. Giving the **NOC** a single correlated view of **interface MTU**, **synthetic DF probes**, **PMTU** learnings, and **NVE** **drop** counters cuts **mean-time** **innocence** for **guest** teams and steers **changes** to the right **transit** **hop** the first time.

## Implementation

Land the five sourcetypes on governed indexes; normalize **`hostname`** / **`device`**; publish **`fabric_mtu_policy.csv`** (site ‚Üí minimum underlay MTU, VXLAN overlay assumptions); **FIELDALIAS** legacy poll fields into **`mtu_value`**, **`df_drops`**, **`pmtu_detected`**, **`vxlan_overhead`**; schedule **`uc_18_3_22_mtu_core`** and page **`uc_18_3_22_mtu_df_risk`** with throttles tied to maintenance windows.

## Evidence

Saved search **`uc_18_3_22_mtu_core`** plus Dashboard Studio panel **MTU/DF risk** with drilldown to raw **`nxos:interface_mtu`** and **`network:ping_results`** events.

## Detailed Implementation

### Prerequisites
- Governed indexes **`dc_fabric`**, **`network_ops`**, and **`network`** with RBAC separating production underlay telemetry from tenant overlay identifiers when privacy policy demands it.
- Heavy Forwarders or automation hosts run Splunk REST API Modular Input ([Splunkbase **1546**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1546)) with HTTPS reachability to **NX-API** and **eAPI** on leaf and spine loopbacks or the management VRF; store secrets in **`passwords.conf`**.
- Use Cisco Data Center Networking for Splunk Platform ([Splunkbase **7777**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/7777)) dashboard JSON as layout reference, but rebind macros to the five sourcetypes named in this UC.
- **Splunk Add-on for Cisco ACI** ([Splunkbase **4022**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4022)) explains APIC-managed faults yet never replaces CLI truth from **`show ip mtu`** and **`show interface`** on the forwarding path.
- **Splunk Add-on for VMware NSX** ([Splunkbase **4856**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4856)) plus **Splunk Add-on for VMware** ([Splunkbase **1810**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1810)) explain vmkernel-originated east-west probes; treat that signal as supplementary unless tests terminate on switch SVIs that model the underlay.
- Maintain **`fabric_mtu_policy.csv`** with columns **`site`**, **`min_underlay_mtu`**, **`vxlan_model`**, and **`suppress_until`**. Baseline documentation assumes IPv4 VXLAN carriage imposes about **50 bytes** of **`vxlan_overhead`**; adjust the planning math when transport is IPv6, when extra labels are stacked, or when packets ride inside WAN encryption.
- Automation accounts require read-only EXEC rights for **`show ip mtu`**, **`show interface`**, **`show nve interface`**, **`show counters`**, and scripted DF **`ping`** from hardened jump hosts.

### Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
- **`nxos:interface_mtu`** ‚Äî Poll NX-API `ins_api` (or an OpenConfig telemetry equivalent) every five minutes, or every sixty seconds during maintenance windows. Emit **`mtu_value`** for each routed fabric or spine-facing interface. Populate **`if_role`** or **`port_usage`** so SPL can skip host-only edge ports that legitimately remain at 1500 bytes.
- **`network:mtu_discovery`** ‚Äî Schedule controller, Ansible Tower, or internal path-MTU jobs between anycast VTEPs or loopbacks. Persist **`pmtu_detected`**, RTT, qualitative **`pmtu_status`**, and explicit **`vxlan_overhead`** when the orchestrator models encapsulation bytes; otherwise let the search default **`vxlan_fill`** to 50.
- **`nxos:nve_counters`** ‚Äî Harvest NVE encap, header, minimum-MTU violation, and DF-style tunnel counters (ASIC naming differs) into numeric **`df_drops`** or composite error tallies such as **`nve_err`**. Expect one compact JSON document per NVE source per interval.
- **`cisco:nxos:syslog`** ‚Äî Forward informational-and-higher facility traffic via SC4S or a Universal Forwarder; normalize ICMP Type 3 Code 4 and related unreachable narratives. Align **`hostname`** with Splunk **`host`** overrides to keep **`device`** keys stable.
- **`network:ping_results`** ‚Äî Automation should execute DF-enabled **`ping`** (Windows **`ping -f`**, Linux **`ping -M do`**) with **`test_size`** between 8900 and 9200 bytes across representative underlay point-to-points. Capture **`df_result`**, loss percentage, and paired traceroute metadata when available.
- Expect only a few megabytes per leaf per day for interface polls plus sparse syslog; unthrottled ping campaigns can dominate volume and may trigger control-plane policers‚Äîcap concurrency.

### Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Save **`uc_18_3_22_mtu_core`**, clone **`uc_18_3_22_mtu_df_risk`** for paging, and wrap index lists with **`macro_dc_fabric_indexes`** so searches port between environments.

```spl
(index=dc_fabric OR index=network_ops OR index=network)
(
  sourcetype="nxos:interface_mtu" OR sourcetype="network:mtu_discovery" OR
  sourcetype="nxos:nve_counters" OR sourcetype="cisco:nxos:syslog" OR
  sourcetype="network:ping_results"
)
earliest=-24h@h latest=now
| eval device=trim(lower(coalesce(hostname,host,switch_id,src_host,nve_src,origin_switch,"")))
| eval interface_name=coalesce(interface_name,if_name,port,s_intf,eth_port,"")
| eval mtu_value=tonumber(coalesce(mtu_value,mtu,ip_mtu,l3_mtu,ipv4_mtu,sys_ip_mtu,if_mtu,""))
| eval vxlan_overhead=tonumber(coalesce(vxlan_overhead,overlay_header_bytes,encap_overhead,vxlan_hdr,vxlan_bytes,""))
| eval vxlan_fill=if(isnull(vxlan_overhead) OR vxlan_overhead<1,50,vxlan_overhead)
| eval df_drops=tonumber(coalesce(df_drops,df_drop_count,dont_fragment_drops,ip_frag_df_drops,nve_df_drops,""))
| eval pmtu_detected=tonumber(coalesce(pmtu_detected,path_mtu,learned_mtu,icmpv4_needfrag_size,icmp_needfrag_mtu,pmtu_bytes,""))
| eval if_role=lower(coalesce(interface_role,if_role,port_role,link_role,port_usage,""))
| eval underlay_hit=if(match(if_role,"uplink|fabric|underlay|leaf|spine|p2p|routed|backbone"),1,0)
| eval mtu_suspect=if(isnotnull(mtu_value) AND mtu_value>0 AND mtu_value<9050 AND underlay_hit==1,1,0)
| eval icmp_needfrag=if(sourcetype=="cisco:nxos:syslog" AND match(_raw,"(?i)(need.?frag|packet.?too.?big|frag.?needed|unreachable.*mtu|icmp.?too.?big|mtu.?exceed)"),1,0)
| eval test_size_n=tonumber(coalesce(test_size,probe_size,echo_bytes,icmp_size,payload_bytes,""))
| eval ping_df_fail=if(sourcetype=="network:ping_results" AND isnotnull(test_size_n) AND test_size_n>=8900 AND match(lower(coalesce(df_result,ping_flags_result,status,probe_outcome,"")),"fail|blackhole|timeout|noreply|unreachable|no.?reply"),1,0)
| eval pmtu_bad=if(sourcetype=="network:mtu_discovery" AND match(lower(coalesce(pmtu_status,state,diag_result,path_health,"")),"blackhole|clamp|loss|icmp|fragmentation|risk|violation|below.?threshold"),1,0)
| eval nve_err=tonumber(coalesce(encap_error,header_err,inval_ucast,inval_mcast,pkts_rx_tunnel_err,min_mtu_violation,tunnel_df_drop,tunnel_frag_drop,mpls_mtu_err,""))
| eval nve_bad=if(sourcetype=="nxos:nve_counters" AND isnotnull(nve_err) AND nve_err>0,1,0)
| eval pmtu_small=if(isnotnull(pmtu_detected) AND pmtu_detected>0 AND pmtu_detected<9000,1,0)
| fillnull value=0 mtu_suspect icmp_needfrag ping_df_fail pmtu_bad nve_bad pmtu_small
| eval score=mtu_suspect*5 + df_drops + icmp_needfrag*4 + ping_df_fail*4 + pmtu_bad*3 + nve_bad*3 + pmtu_small*4
| where mtu_suspect==1 OR df_drops>0 OR icmp_needfrag==1 OR ping_df_fail==1 OR pmtu_bad==1 OR nve_bad==1 OR pmtu_small==1
| bin _time span=1h
| stats sum(df_drops) AS df_drop_sum max(mtu_value) AS mtu_report max(pmtu_detected) AS pmtu_report max(vxlan_fill) AS vxlan_oh values(sourcetype) AS sourcetypes_seen latest(interface_name) AS if_last count AS event_samples BY _time device
| eval story=device."|mtu=".tostring(round(mtu_report))."|pmtu=".tostring(round(pmtu_report))."|vxoh=".tostring(round(vxlan_oh))."|df=".tostring(round(df_drop_sum))
| sort - df_drop_sum - event_samples
| head 150
| table _time device mtu_report pmtu_report vxlan_oh df_drop_sum sourcetypes_seen if_last event_samples story
```

#### Understanding this SPL ‚Äî The search unions **`nxos:interface_mtu`**, **`network:mtu_discovery`**, **`nxos:nve_counters`**, **`cisco:nxos:syslog`**, and **`network:ping_results`** without joins. The `coalesce()` calls absorb renames across NX-OS maintenance trains. `vxlan_fill` guarantees a numeric overlay header budget when **`vxlan_overhead`** is absent. `mtu_suspect` enforces the 9050-byte underlay floor for typical IPv4 VXLAN fabric links; raise that constant if architecture mandates more headroom for additional encapsulation. `score` emphasizes interface-level misses and counter-based DF drops while still elevating syslog ICMP hints. One-hour bins damp micro-bursts yet keep escalation cadence predictable.

**Pipeline walkthrough** ‚Äî Restrict indexes; normalize **`device`**; cast **`mtu_value`**, **`pmtu_detected`**, and **`df_drops`**; classify underlay interfaces; mark syslog, probe, and discovery failures; filter to rows with any risk flag; aggregate per hour and device; emit the compact **`story`** string for tickets.

### Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
- On two witness leaf switches run **`show ip mtu`**, **`show interface`**, and **`show nve interface`**; confirm Splunk **`mtu_value`** matches CLI output after accounting for subinterface inheritance versus parent port MTU. Cross-check the Cisco Nexus Interfaces Configuration Guide section on MTU for platform-specific nuances.
- From the same automation host feeding **`network:ping_results`**, run manual DF sweeps; Splunk **`df_result`** must match CLI outcomes for the same **`test_size_n`**.
- Compare orchestrated **`pmtu_detected`** values with controller path diagnostics or Linux **`tracepath`** captured in the same timestamp window.
- **`df_drop_sum`** should climb only when DF-marked traffic crosses a known small-MTU lab link; otherwise re-map ASIC counters feeding **`nve_err`**.
- Plot **`timechart count` by sourcetype**; post-upgrade stair steps usually indicate token rotation or SC4S filter edits rather than fabric decay.

### Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
- Executive row: single-value devices impacted per site with a trailing twenty-four-hour sparkline.
- NOC row: sortable table keyed on **`story`**, **`mtu_report`**, **`pmtu_report`**, **`df_drop_sum`**, and **`sourcetypes_seen`** for rapid signal attribution.
- Engineering row: overlay **`pmtu_report`** against **`vxlan_oh`** by POD with change-ticket markers from your CMDB integration.
- Access controls: scrub customer VRF names from exports while retaining internal point-to-point identifiers for engineering drilldowns.

### Step 5 ‚Äî Visualization, alerting, and troubleshooting
**Visualization** ‚Äî Combine a heatmap of **`device`**, hour, and score with line charts for **`pmtu_report`**. Dashboard Studio single-value tiles can track **`df_drop_sum`** peaks. When topology data exists, map failing **`network:ping_results`** vectors onto underlay graphs for segment isolation.

**Alert design** ‚Äî Schedule every fifteen minutes across **`earliest=-24h@h`**. Page when **`df_drop_sum`** increases between consecutive evaluations for the same device, when **`event_samples`** from **`network:ping_results`** exceeds a tuned floor while **`mtu_report`** stays below **9050**, or when **`sourcetypes_seen`** includes **`network:mtu_discovery`** with **`pmtu_report`** under **9000**‚Äîafter honoring **`suppress_until`** in **`fabric_mtu_policy.csv`**. Throttle forty-five minutes per device; payload includes **`story`**, **`mtu_report`**, **`pmtu_report`**, **`vxlan_oh`**, **`if_last`**, and links to **7777** reference panels‚Äînever credentials.

**Troubleshooting** ‚Äî **`mtu_report`** near **9216** with ongoing ping failures suggests an off-box choke point (firewall, load balancer, MPLS core) outside the VXLAN underlay; extend **`network:mtu_discovery`** coverage or ingest parallel WAN sourcetypes. **`icmp_needfrag`** without **`mtu_suspect`** may be mis-tagged edge traffic‚Äîuse ingestion IP metadata before reassigning ownership. After supervisor failover, empty **`nxos:nve_counters`** usually means stale REST targets rather than healed fabric. If application throughput remains poor while counters stay zero, plan SPAN or ERSPAN captures because some ASIC paths still drop silently.

## SPL

```spl
(index=dc_fabric OR index=network_ops OR index=network)
(
  sourcetype="nxos:interface_mtu" OR sourcetype="network:mtu_discovery" OR
  sourcetype="nxos:nve_counters" OR sourcetype="cisco:nxos:syslog" OR
  sourcetype="network:ping_results"
)
earliest=-24h@h latest=now
| eval device=trim(lower(coalesce(hostname,host,switch_id,src_host,nve_src,origin_switch,"")))
| eval interface_name=coalesce(interface_name,if_name,port,s_intf,eth_port,"")
| eval mtu_value=tonumber(coalesce(mtu_value,mtu,ip_mtu,l3_mtu,ipv4_mtu,sys_ip_mtu,if_mtu,""))
| eval vxlan_overhead=tonumber(coalesce(vxlan_overhead,overlay_header_bytes,encap_overhead,vxlan_hdr,vxlan_bytes,""))
| eval vxlan_fill=if(isnull(vxlan_overhead) OR vxlan_overhead<1,50,vxlan_overhead)
| eval df_drops=tonumber(coalesce(df_drops,df_drop_count,dont_fragment_drops,ip_frag_df_drops,nve_df_drops,""))
| eval pmtu_detected=tonumber(coalesce(pmtu_detected,path_mtu,learned_mtu,icmpv4_needfrag_size,icmp_needfrag_mtu,pmtu_bytes,""))
| eval if_role=lower(coalesce(interface_role,if_role,port_role,link_role,port_usage,""))
| eval underlay_hit=if(match(if_role,"uplink|fabric|underlay|leaf|spine|p2p|routed|backbone"),1,0)
| eval mtu_suspect=if(isnotnull(mtu_value) AND mtu_value>0 AND mtu_value<9050 AND underlay_hit==1,1,0)
| eval icmp_needfrag=if(sourcetype=="cisco:nxos:syslog" AND match(_raw,"(?i)(need.?frag|packet.?too.?big|frag.?needed|unreachable.*mtu|icmp.?too.?big|mtu.?exceed)"),1,0)
| eval test_size_n=tonumber(coalesce(test_size,probe_size,echo_bytes,icmp_size,payload_bytes,""))
| eval ping_df_fail=if(sourcetype=="network:ping_results" AND isnotnull(test_size_n) AND test_size_n>=8900 AND match(lower(coalesce(df_result,ping_flags_result,status,probe_outcome,"")),"fail|blackhole|timeout|noreply|unreachable|no.?reply"),1,0)
| eval pmtu_bad=if(sourcetype=="network:mtu_discovery" AND match(lower(coalesce(pmtu_status,state,diag_result,path_health,"")),"blackhole|clamp|loss|icmp|fragmentation|risk|violation|below.?threshold"),1,0)
| eval nve_err=tonumber(coalesce(encap_error,header_err,inval_ucast,inval_mcast,pkts_rx_tunnel_err,min_mtu_violation,tunnel_df_drop,tunnel_frag_drop,mpls_mtu_err,""))
| eval nve_bad=if(sourcetype=="nxos:nve_counters" AND isnotnull(nve_err) AND nve_err>0,1,0)
| eval pmtu_small=if(isnotnull(pmtu_detected) AND pmtu_detected>0 AND pmtu_detected<9000,1,0)
| fillnull value=0 mtu_suspect icmp_needfrag ping_df_fail pmtu_bad nve_bad pmtu_small
| eval score=mtu_suspect*5 + df_drops + icmp_needfrag*4 + ping_df_fail*4 + pmtu_bad*3 + nve_bad*3 + pmtu_small*4
| where mtu_suspect==1 OR df_drops>0 OR icmp_needfrag==1 OR ping_df_fail==1 OR pmtu_bad==1 OR nve_bad==1 OR pmtu_small==1
| bin _time span=1h
| stats sum(df_drops) AS df_drop_sum max(mtu_value) AS mtu_report max(pmtu_detected) AS pmtu_report max(vxlan_fill) AS vxlan_oh values(sourcetype) AS sourcetypes_seen latest(interface_name) AS if_last count AS event_samples BY _time device
| eval story=device."|mtu=".tostring(round(mtu_report))."|pmtu=".tostring(round(pmtu_report))."|vxoh=".tostring(round(vxlan_oh))."|df=".tostring(round(df_drop_sum))
| sort - df_drop_sum - event_samples
| head 150
| table _time device mtu_report pmtu_report vxlan_oh df_drop_sum sourcetypes_seen if_last event_samples story
```

## Visualization

(1) **Table** ‚Äî worst **`device`** rows with **`story`**, **`mtu_report`**, **`pmtu_report`**, **`df_drop_sum`**, **`sourcetypes_seen`**. (2) **Choropleth** / **site** **matrix** ‚Äî share of paths failing **DF** probes by **POD**. (3) **Single value** ‚Äî count of **`device`**/`hour` with **`score`** signals. (4) **Overlay line** ‚Äî **`pmtu_report`** trend vs **`vxlan_oh`**. (5) **Sankey-style** flow (Dashboard Studio) ‚Äî **`network:ping_results`** source leaf ‚Üí failing hop inferred from **`network:mtu_discovery`**.

## Known False Positives

**maintenance_mtu_reboot** ‚Äî During **ISSU** or **supervisor** **switchover**, **`nxos:interface_mtu`** polls may return **transient** **defaults** while **interfaces** **reconcile**, creating a **short** spike in **`mtu_suspect`**. Require **two** clean **polls** post **change** **record**, or honor **`suppress_until`** in **`fabric_mtu_policy.csv`** before paging.

**icmp_rate_limited** ‚Äî **DF** **pings** at **aggressive** **cadence** can trigger **control-plane** **policers** that **mimic** **blackholes** in **`network:ping_results`**. Space **probes**, randomize **starts**, and compare against **`show control-plane`** drop counters‚Äîcorrelation distinguishes policing from true MTU faults.

**lab_port_wrong_role** ‚Äî **Automation** sometimes tags **host-facing** ports as **underlay** due to **stale** **CMDB** **role** strings, so **`mtu_suspect`** fires on **access** segments that **intentionally** stay at **1500**. Refresh **`if_role`** from **source-of-truth** **YAML**, and exclude **edge** **templates** with **`port_usage=host`.

**guest_mss_clamp_only** ‚Äî **`pmtu_bad`** may reflect **hypervisor** **MSS** **clamps** or **NSX** **policy**, not **underlay** **fragmentation**. When **`4856`**/**`1810`** context shows **VM-only** paths, reroute **evidence** to **compute** owners unless **`nxos:interface_mtu`** also regresses.

**syslog_echo_noise** ‚Äî **Benign** **ICMP** messages during **DDD** **debugs** can match **`icmp_needfrag`** **regexes**. Tighten **facility** **filters**, require **`%ETHPORT-`** or **`%L3FM-`** **tokens** where **platform** **supports** them, or **AND** with **`mtu_suspect`** before **sev-2** routes.

**false_positive_nve_map** ‚Äî **Telemetry** **templates** occasionally map **unrelated** counters into **`encap_error`**, inflating **`nve_bad`**. After **ASIC** **driver** bumps, reconcile JSON paths with **`show counters`** differences on a witness leaf.

**df_probe_asymmetry** ‚Äî **ECMP** **hashing** may steer **DF** **pings** down a **healthy** path while **tenant** flows hit the **low-MTU** **link** until **`network:mtu_discovery`** rotates **probes**. Increase **probe** **diversity** (multiple **sources**, **flows**, **UDP** **templates**) before closing **tickets**.

## References

- [RFC 8926 ‚Äî Virtual eXtensible Local Area Network (VXLAN): A Framework for Overlaying Virtualized Layer 2 Networks over Layer 3 Networks](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc8926)
- [RFC 1191 ‚Äî Path MTU Discovery](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc1191)
- [Cisco Nexus 9000 Series NX-OS Interfaces Configuration Guide ‚Äî Configuring Maximum Transmission Units](https://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/td/docs/dcn/nx-os/nexus9000/102x/configuration/interfaces/cisco-nexus-9000-nx-os-interfaces-configuration-guide-release-102x/m-configuring-maximum-transfer-units.pdf)
- [Splunk CIM ‚Äî Network Traffic data model](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/NetworkTraffic)
- [Splunk Add-on for Cisco ACI (Splunkbase 4022)](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4022)
- [Splunk REST API Modular Input (Splunkbase 1546)](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1546)
- [Cisco Data Center Networking for Splunk Platform (Splunkbase 7777)](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/7777)
- [Splunk Add-on for VMware NSX (Splunkbase 4856)](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4856)
- [Splunk Add-on for VMware (Splunkbase 1810)](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1810)
